arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-objdump 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading libcoreclr.so debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# Build ID: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try the ELF build-id format for symbol file
curl -sS -L -o /tmp/libcoreclr_sym.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1 | tail -3
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The key frames are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with that specific Build ID
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses to function names

The Build ID is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. This is a .NET runtime library, so the symbols might be available from Microsoft's symbol server.


I'm working out the correct URL format for Microsoft's symbol server to fetch the debug symbols for this ELF binary. The pattern for Linux symbols appears to involve the filename and build ID, so I'll try constructing the proper request to download the symbols.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try various Microsoft symbol server URL formats
for path in \
  "libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  "_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  "libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg" \
  "libcoreclr.so.dwarf/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dwarf" \
  "libcoreclr.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.dbg"; do
  code=$(curl -sS -L -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${path}")
  echo "$code  $path"
done
arguments.description: Try various symbol server URL formats

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try various symbol server URL formats
